architecture.summary: RT is a few-shot NER framework that combines retrieval and chain-of-thought reasoning on top of a larger language model. A retrieval module identifies relevant in-context examples from a demonstration pool for each input sentence; those examples are fed as context to the LLM. A chain-of-thought module then guides the LLM through systematic reasoning steps to identify and label biomedical entities in the input.

biblio.title: RT: a Retrieving and Chain-of-Thought framework for few-shot medical named entity recognition
biblio.venue: Journal of the American Medical Informatics Association

claims.statement: Our proposed RT framework outperforms other LMs for few-shot NER tasks with micro-F1 scores of 93.50 and 91.76 on BC5CDR and NCBI corpora, respectively
Our proposed RT framework outperforms other LMs for few-shot NER tasks with micro-F1 scores of 93.50 and 91.76 on BC5CDR and NCBI corpora, respectively
RT framework outperforms 16 other models for few-shot NER tasks
Chain-of-Thought performed better than Tree-of-Thought when using both positive and negative samples
utilization of a partially annotated dataset has a marginal effect on model performance
This is the first investigation to combine a retrieval-based LLM and Chain-of-Thought methodology to enhance the performance in biomedical few-shot NER
